steps.title: Identify the central contrast
steps.content: The phrase “But then begins a journey in my head” marks the main shift—the speaker intends to rest, but his mind becomes active instead.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the answers
steps.content:
The passage shows the speaker thinks about the friend instead of sleeping—”drooping eyelids open wide” confirms he's awake.

D matches this exactly. A and B misread the mental journey as actual sleep or travel; C introduces a discussion absent from the text.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
